name: Goldstar Transport
principals: Donald Smith, Donna Smith (founders); Kristie Custis (Manager)
service_types: non-emergency medical transport (NEMT), hospice/palliative transport; NOT school bus
districts_or_customers: Salem-area hospitals, hospice and palliative care providers in Willamette Valley
recent_news: Small NEMT operator founded 2006 by Donald & Donna Smith; ~9 vehicles per company materials. No M&A activity identified.
fit_notes: NOT a Summit tuck-in fit. This is a non-emergency medical transport company in Salem OR (distinct from the unrelated 'GoldStar Transit' school operator under STA). Out of scope for student transportation roll-up.

name: Suburban School Transportation
principals: James Ondrejcak (Owner); Sue B. (Operations/HR Mgr); Barb W. (Transportation Logistics Mgr); Austin O. (Fleet Mgr). Note: 'Arthur Bernard / Waltham MA' in source data appears to be a data error - Arthur Bernard is President of Airport Coach Ltd. (Waltham, MA), an unrelated airport/corporate transport company. Actual SSTN is headquartered in Brunswick, OH.
service_types: K-12 school bus (public & private schools), special needs transportation, wheelchair/modified vans, after-school programs
districts_or_customers: 75+ school districts across 12 counties in Northeastern Ohio; transports ~900 students daily
recent_news: Long-standing independent NE Ohio contractor in business 35+ years; no public M&A activity identified through 2025-2026.
fit_notes: Strong potential tuck-in for Summit: independent owner-operated NE Ohio school/SPED contractor, ~225-vehicle fleet, 75+ districts, multi-decade operating history. Ohio is a Summit/Durham geography. Worth diligence on succession (Ondrejcak as sole owner) and contract retention.

name: Monark Student Transportation Corp
principals: Mark D. Schmitt (President/Owner); Connie Davis (Manager). Affiliated companies: Blue Bird Bus Sales of Pittsburgh/Virginia/WV, Qualistar Transportation, Qualistar/Qualimark Fleet Service.
service_types: contracted K-12 school bus, special needs, fleet management, CDL testing (Class A/B/C), proprietary 9-passenger SBCV vehicles
districts_or_customers: West Allegheny, Quaker Valley, Grove City Area, Hampton, North Catholic, Pittsburgh Public Schools, Crafton, Fort Cherry, Gibsonia, Union City SDs across Western PA
recent_news: Locally owned 30+ years; operates 300+ vehicles across Western PA. Allied News (2021) coverage on Grove City busing challenges; no M&A activity disclosed.
fit_notes: Highly attractive Summit tuck-in candidate: Schmitt-family-owned PA platform with ~300 buses, multiple districts including Pittsburgh, and affiliated Blue Bird dealer/Qualistar tour operations. PA is Summit core geography. Multi-entity family business could be split (operating co vs. dealer) in transaction. Diligence on Schmitt succession.

name: Taylor Motors Inc
principals: Taylor family - John Tom Taylor & Della Taylor (founders, 1961); Tommye D. Taylor (long-time owner, d. 2014); David Taylor (current owner/manager of Taylor Bus Sales). New CEO appointed June 2023 for motorcoach/tour/charter operations (Lane Report).
service_types: school bus operations (KY, TN, NC, SC, GA, AL); motorcoach/charter/tour (Louisville hub); shuttle; wheelchair-accessible coaches; DOD-approved military charters; also a Blue Bird/Diamond/Collins/Pegasus bus dealer (Taylor Bus Sales)
districts_or_customers: Southeastern U.S. school districts across KY/TN/NC/SC/GA/AL; military DOD contracts; corporate shuttles; tour groups
recent_news: June 2023: New CEO appointed at Taylor Motors travel/tour/charter division (Lane Report). Family-owned since 1961; founder Tommye Taylor died 2014, business continues under next generation. Company HQ is Murray, KY (note: source data 'Fort Benning, GA' address appears to be a satellite/contract location, not HQ).
fit_notes: Mixed fit: Taylor Motors is a family-owned multi-state school bus + motorcoach operator (~200 vehicles) across 6 southeastern states, plus an integrated bus dealership (Taylor Bus Sales). Succession dynamics (3rd-gen, 2023 outside CEO hire) suggest possible sale appetite. Summit would primarily value school bus ops and would need to carve out or take dealer/charter as adjacency. Geographic expansion play (non-Summit core states).

name: Able Bus Inc
principals: Anthony J. Romanelli (Owner per 2013 NYC DOE contractor list); Joanne Romanelli (Owner per source data, likely family member/spouse)
service_types: NYC DOE-contracted yellow school bus transportation
districts_or_customers: New York City Department of Education (NYC public schools), Brooklyn-based routes
recent_news: Long-standing NYC DOE school bus contractor (on 2013 OPT contractor list). NYC school bus contract environment turbulent in 2025: October emergency 30-day contract, November 2025 Panel for Educational Policy approved 3-year extension (Chalkbeat). NYCSBUS acquired Reliant 2020 but no public indication Able Bus has been acquired.
fit_notes: Romanelli-family-owned NYC DOE yellow bus contractor in Brooklyn. NYC is highly regulated (OPT contracts, union/ATU 1181 dynamics, EPP retirement liabilities) which complicates tuck-ins; NYC DOE has aggressively in-housed via NYCSBUS. Summit fit is modest - NYC market consolidation is constrained. Better candidate for opportunistic acquisition if owner succession surfaces; verify operating status and fleet via OPT directly.
